2603.26259.title: Working Notes on Late Interaction Dynamics: Analyzing Targeted Behaviors of Late Interaction Models
2603.26259.paper_summary: While Late Interaction models exhibit strong retrieval performance, many of their underlying dynamics remain understudied, potentially hiding performance bottlenecks. In this work, we focus on two topics in Late Interaction retrieval: a length bias that arises when using multi-vector scoring, and the similarity distribution beyond the best scores pooled by the MaxSim operator. We analyze these behaviors for state-of-the-art models on the NanoBEIR benchmark. Results show that while the theoretical length bias of causal Late Interaction models holds in practice, bi-directional models can also suffer from it in extreme cases. We also note that no significant similarity trend lies beyond the top-1 document token, validating that the MaxSim operator efficiently exploits the token-level similarity scores.
2603.26259.paper_summary_zh: 雖然晚期互動模型展現了強大的檢索性能，但其許多潛在動態仍然未被充分研究，這可能隱藏了性能瓶頸。<lb/>在這項工作中，我們專注於晚期互動檢索中的兩個主題：使用多向量評分時出現的長度偏差，以及超越 MaxSim 操作符所聚合的最佳分數的相似性分佈。<lb/>我們分析了這些行為在 NanoBEIR 基準上的最先進模型。<lb/>結果顯示，儘管因果晚期互動模型的理論長度偏差在實踐中成立，但雙向模型在極端情況下也可能受到影響。<lb/>我們還注意到，在前 1 個文檔標記之外沒有顯著的相似性趨勢，這驗證了 MaxSim 操作符有效地利用了標記級相似性分數。
2603.26259.author: Antoine Edy et.al.
2603.26259.authors: Antoine Edy, Max Conti, Quentin Macé
